evals.prompt: I want to set up my product marketing context. We're a B2B SaaS company that sells a customer feedback platform to product teams.
evals.expected_output: Should inspect the authenticated workspace brief and productMarketingContext already supplied by the server. If no product marketing context exists, should offer two options: (1) Auto-draft from workspace evidence (recommended) or (2) Start from scratch. If the user chooses start from scratch, should walk through sections conversationally one at a time. Should cover all applicable sections: Product Overview, Target Audience, Personas, Problems You Solve, Competitive Landscape, Differentiation, Objections, Switching Dynamics, Customer Language, Brand Voice, Proof Points, and Goals. Should present the completed sections and request approval for update_product_marketing_context when ready to persist them.
assertions: Checks the authenticated workspace for existing product marketing context
Offers two options: auto-draft or start from scratch
Covers applicable sections
Walks through sections conversationally one at a time
Uses update_product_marketing_context after user review

evals.prompt: Update our product marketing context. We just added a new enterprise tier and our target audience has expanded to include VP of Engineering, not just Product Managers.
evals.expected_output: Should read the existing productMarketingContext from the authenticated workspace brief. Should identify which sections need updating based on the changes: Target Audience (add VP of Engineering), Personas (add new persona), Product Overview (new enterprise tier, including pricing updates within that section), Objections (enterprise-specific), and Competitive Landscape (enterprise competitors). Should present only those proposed changes, preserve unchanged sections, and request approval for update_product_marketing_context.
assertions: Reads existing workspace product marketing context
Identifies sections that need updating
Updates Target Audience with VP of Engineering
Adds new persona for the expanded audience
Updates Product Overview for enterprise tier
Preserves unchanged sections through a partial workspace update

evals.prompt: create a product context doc for my app. it's a mobile app that helps people find hiking trails. we're just getting started.
evals.expected_output: Should trigger on casual phrasing. Should check for existing context doc. Should offer auto-draft or start-from-scratch options. Should adapt questions for an early-stage B2C mobile app (outdoor/fitness niche). Should note that some sections may be sparse for an early-stage product and that's okay — they can be filled in as the business matures. Should skip non-applicable sections (e.g., Personas section is B2B-focused) rather than forcing all 12. Should accept lighter answers for sections like Proof Points or Competitive Landscape if the company is new.
assertions: Triggers on casual phrasing
Checks for existing context doc
Offers auto-draft or start-from-scratch options
Adapts questions for early-stage B2C mobile app
Notes some sections may be sparse early on
Skips non-applicable sections rather than forcing all 12
Uses the authenticated workspace profile instead of a filesystem file

evals.prompt: Can you auto-draft our product marketing context from our workspace and marketing materials?
evals.expected_output: Should activate the auto-draft workflow mode. Should start from the authenticated workspace brief, website, existing product marketing sections, and current Context.dev evidence where needed. Should draft the product marketing sections from supported information, flag assumptions and sections that need manual input, and present the draft for review before requesting approval to persist it.
assertions: Activates auto-draft workflow mode
Uses workspace and current website evidence
Drafts context from found information
Flags sections needing manual input
Presents draft for review before requesting persistence approval

evals.prompt: Do we have a product marketing context set up? I want to make sure the other marketing skills have context about our product.
evals.expected_output: Should inspect productMarketingContext in the authenticated workspace brief. Should report whether it exists and summarize its contents if found without exposing raw profile fields. If it doesn't exist, should offer to create it and explain why it is valuable. Should explain that the root passes relevant workspace context to the specialist subagents.
assertions: Checks the authenticated workspace profile
Reports whether product marketing context exists
Summarizes contents if found
Offers to create if missing
Explains how other skills use it

evals.prompt: Write homepage copy for our SaaS product.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is a copywriting task, not a product marketing context update. Should use the current authenticated workspace brief and may suggest initializing missing product marketing context first, but should delegate the actual homepage copy to the copy-creative specialist.
assertions: Recognizes this as a copywriting task
May check for or suggest initializing workspace product marketing context
References or delegates to the copy-creative specialist for the actual copy
Does not attempt to write homepage copy using context creation patterns
